task: A teammate saved an OpenAPI spec with a `.json` extension but the file is actually YAML. I run `smithers openapi list ./spec.json`. Will it still parse, and how does Smithers decide JSON vs YAML and what minimal fields must the parsed content have before Smithers accepts it as a spec?
notes: Correct answer: parseSpecText ignores the file extension and sniffs the content — it tries JSON.parse first, and on failure falls back to yaml.parse, so a YAML file with a .json name still parses. It then validates the parsed value is a non-null object containing (openapi OR swagger) AND (paths OR info); otherwise it throws 'Failed to parse OpenAPI spec as JSON or YAML' or 'does not appear to be a valid OpenAPI spec'.

task: I'm calling loadSpecSync with an `https://...` URL to my spec and it errors as if the URL were a bad file. Why does the sync loader not fetch the URL, and which loader should I use for a remote spec?
notes: Correct answer: loadSpecSync (and createOpenApiToolsSync) never fetch URLs — they readFileSync the string and, if that fails, parse the string itself as raw JSON/YAML text, so an http(s) URL gets parsed as raw text and fails validation. For remote URLs use the async/Effect path: loadSpecEffect or createOpenApiTools, which fetch http(s) URLs.

task: Which exported function loads an OpenAPI spec from an object, file path, raw text, OR a remote URL and returns an Effect (so it can fetch http(s) URLs)? Answer with just the function name.
When jsonSchemaToZod hits a schema it can't cleanly represent (no type, no enum, no allOf/anyOf/oneOf), what Zod type does it fall back to? Answer with just the Zod type.

My spec has a `$ref` pointing at another file like `./common.yaml#/components/schemas/Pet`, and one schema references itself. Does Smithers' OpenAPI $ref resolver follow external/remote refs, and what happens with a circular $ref?
notes: Correct answer: resolveRef only resolves LOCAL JSON pointers that start with '#/' within the same spec document; any other form (external file, remote URL) throws 'Unsupported $ref format'. Circular references are detected via a visited set in jsonSchemaToZod and bail out to z.any() (described as a circular reference) rather than recursing forever. Must NOT claim it fetches external files or follows remote $refs.

task: Write a TypeScript snippet that builds AI SDK tools from a REMOTE OpenAPI spec at https://api.acme.dev/openapi.json, restricted to only the `listInvoices` and `getInvoice` operations, authenticated with a bearer token from process.env.ACME_TOKEN, then passes the resulting tools to an AnthropicAgent.
canonicalAnswer: await createOpenApiTools('https://api.acme.dev/openapi.json', { auth: { type: 'bearer', token: process.env.ACME_TOKEN }, include: ['listInvoices','getInvoice'] }); then new AnthropicAgent({ model, tools })
notes: Correct code must use the ASYNC createOpenApiTools (awaited) because the spec is a remote URL; the sync variant createOpenApiToolsSync cannot fetch URLs and is a wrong answer. Options must include auth:{type:'bearer',token:process.env.ACME_TOKEN} and include:['listInvoices','getInvoice'] (operationId allowlist). The returned record of tools is then spread into an SDK agent: new AnthropicAgent({ model, tools }).

task: The `smithers openapi generate` CLI and createOpenApiToolsSync are synchronous. For which kinds of spec input can I NOT use the sync tool factory, and why?
notes: Correct answer: createOpenApiToolsSync only works when the spec input is an already-parsed object or a LOCAL file path (or raw text). It cannot be used with an http(s) URL because fetching a remote spec is asynchronous; for URLs you must use the async createOpenApiTools. The CLI `openapi generate` therefore takes a local spec path.

task: I want to audit which endpoints an agent will be able to call before wiring up tools, using the programmatic listOperations(input). What four fields does it return per operation, and is the HTTP method upper- or lower-cased?
notes: Correct answer: listOperations returns an array of { operationId, method, path, summary }. The method is UPPER-cased (op.method.toUpperCase(), e.g. GET/POST) even though the internal HttpMethod type stores lowercase. listOperations loads the spec synchronously and maps over extractOperations.

task: With auth { type: 'bearer', token }, which HTTP header does Smithers set on every request, and what prefix precedes the token value?
How does Smithers turn auth { type: 'basic', username, password } into a request header? Be specific about the encoding.
notes: Correct answer: buildAuthHeaders sets the Authorization header to 'Basic ' + btoa(`${username}:${password}`) — i.e. the base64 encoding of `username:password`. Must mention base64 (btoa) of the colon-joined credentials and the 'Basic' scheme, not 'Bearer'.

task: An API wants its key sent as a query parameter named `api_key`, not a header. Give the OpenApiToolsOptions.auth config, and explain where Smithers actually puts the key for in:'query' versus in:'header'.
notes: Correct answer: auth: { type: 'apiKey', name: 'api_key', in: 'query', value: <key> }. For in:'query', buildUrl appends the key to the URL's searchParams (fullUrl.searchParams.set(name, value)). For in:'header', buildAuthHeaders sets a request header whose name is `name` and value is `value`. The `in` field switches between query-string and header placement.

task: I generated tools from a 60-endpoint spec but only want the agent to see the 3 inventory read endpoints. Which option restricts the surface, does it match on operationId or on the generated/prefixed tool name, and what happens if I also set exclude?
notes: Correct answer: use include: [operationId, ...] as an allowlist. include/exclude always match the ORIGINAL operationId, never the curated name or the namePrefix-prefixed display name. If both include and exclude are set, exclude wins. Per-operation curation (operations[id] = false / { include: false }) can also drop endpoints.

task: For an operation GET /pets/{petId}, how does Smithers place the petId argument into the request URL, and is the value escaped?
notes: Correct answer: path parameters are substituted into the literal `{petId}` placeholder in the path via replaceAll, and each value is URL-encoded with encodeURIComponent before substitution. The arg is sorted into the path bucket by its in:'path' designation in executeRequest.

task: How are query parameters serialized onto the request URL, and does Smithers honor OpenAPI parameter serialization styles like `deepObject` or comma-explode?
notes: Correct answer: query parameters are set with default URLSearchParams serialization (fullUrl.searchParams.set(key, String(value))). OpenAPI parameter serialization styles (style/explode, deepObject, comma-separated arrays, etc.) are NOT implemented — only default key=value serialization is done.

task: After my agent calls an OpenAPI tool I filter the run event log for an `OpenApiToolCalled` event and see nothing. Do OpenAPI tool calls emit run-event-bus events today, and how ARE the calls observable?
mustNot: emits OpenApiToolCalled onto the run event bus
notes: Correct answer: the tool factory does NOT emit OpenApiToolCalled onto the Smithers run event bus today (that event variant is typed/categorized for future integration). Calls are observable via the exported Effect metrics openApiToolCallsTotal, openApiToolCallErrorsTotal, and openApiToolDuration (a histogram), plus Effect log annotations/spans carrying the operation id, method, and path.

task: Besides JSON, what other text format can Smithers parse natively when loading an OpenAPI spec from a file or raw string? Answer with one word.

A confused (or hostile) spec declares an `Authorization` HTTP *header parameter* on an operation, so the LLM could supply its own Authorization value when calling the tool. Can an LLM-supplied header parameter override or strip the operator-injected bearer token? Explain the ordering.
mustNot: the LLM header overrides the injected auth
notes: Correct answer: No. executeRequest builds headers as { ...headerParams, ...buildAuthHeaders(options) } — the LLM-controlled header PARAMETERS are spread FIRST and the operator-injected auth/headers are spread LAST, so the injected Authorization (or apiKey header / custom headers) always wins and can never be overridden or stripped by an LLM-supplied header param. This is a deliberate trust boundary.

task: By default, under what single argument key does a generated OpenAPI tool expect the JSON request body (assuming no parameter already uses that name)? Answer with just the key.
If I don't pass a baseUrl option, where does Smithers get the request base URL, and what is used if the spec has no `servers` entry at all?
notes: Correct answer: resolveBaseUrl returns options.baseUrl when provided; otherwise spec.servers[0].url (the first server); otherwise it falls back to 'http://localhost'. An explicit baseUrl overrides the spec's servers.

task: I pass headers: { 'X-Tenant': 'acme' } in OpenApiToolsOptions. Are these sent on every generated tool's request, and relative to the auth header which one wins on a name collision?
notes: Correct answer: options.headers are merged into EVERY request. In buildAuthHeaders the auth header is set first and then Object.assign(headers, options.headers) applies the custom headers last, so custom headers override the auth header on a name collision; and the whole buildAuthHeaders result is applied after (over) the LLM header params in executeRequest.

task: Which OpenApiToolsOptions field prefixes every generated tool name (e.g. to namespace `github_` in front of each operationId-derived name)? Answer with just the field name.
An operation `GET /pets/{petId}` in the spec has no `operationId`. What operationId/tool name does Smithers auto-generate for it? Answer with just the generated id.
What happens if I call createOpenApiTool(spec, 'doesNotExist') with an operationId that isn't in the spec, or one that's been excluded by curation?
notes: Correct answer: createOpenApiTool throws. For an unknown operationId it throws `Operation "doesNotExist" not found in spec. Available: <comma-separated list of operationIds>`. For an operationId excluded via include/exclude/operations curation it throws `Operation "..." is excluded by OpenAPI tool curation options.` It does not silently return undefined or an empty tool.

task: An endpoint responds with text/plain or text/csv (not JSON). What does the generated tool hand back to the agent — a parsed object or a raw string — and how is that decided?
notes: Correct answer: the tool inspects the response Content-Type header. Only when it includes 'application/json' is the body parsed via response.json() into an object; for everything else (text/plain, text/csv, etc.) the body is returned as a string via response.text(). So non-JSON responses come back as strings.

task: When an OpenAPI tool's HTTP call fails or the server returns a 500, does the generated tool throw and abort the agent loop, or return something the agent can read? What is the shape, and is the secret auth header at risk of leaking in the error?
notes: Correct answer: the tool's execute() catches the error and RETURNS { error: true, message, status: 'failed' } instead of throwing, so the agent can react in its loop. Non-2xx responses are first turned into an Error (`HTTP <status> <statusText>: <body>`, with status/body attached) inside executeRequest and then caught and wrapped. The error message never embeds the RequestInit, so the injected Authorization/secret header cannot leak.

task: jsonSchemaToZod supports allOf, anyOf, and oneOf. Which of these maps to a Zod intersection and which map to a Zod union?
notes: Correct answer: allOf is merged into a Zod INTERSECTION (z.intersection across the sub-schemas, single entry passes through). oneOf and anyOf both become a Zod UNION (z.union of the variant schemas). Must not swap these (allOf is not a union; oneOf/anyOf are not intersections).

task: Does the OpenAPI-to-Zod conversion preserve a field's `nullable: true` and its `default` value? Which Zod modifiers get applied?
notes: Correct answer: yes, both are preserved. nullable:true applies .nullable() (maybeNullable), and a present `default` applies .default(value) (maybeDefault) to the generated Zod schema. A spec `description` is also carried over via .describe().

task: Using the `operations` curation option of createOpenApiTools, expose ONLY `createOrder` and `cancelOrder` from a large spec: rename createOrder's generated tool to `place_order`, give it an agent-facing description, attach a 201 responseExample, and make sure no other endpoint becomes a tool. Show the OpenApiToolsOptions you'd pass.
canonicalAnswer: { include: ['createOrder','cancelOrder'], operations: { createOrder: { name: 'place_order', description: '...', responseExamples: [{ status: 201, value: { id: 'ord_1' } }] } } }
notes: Correct answer: the operations record is keyed by the ORIGINAL operationId (createOrder), where name renames the generated tool ('place_order'), description replaces the spec summary/description, and responseExamples (array of { status, description?, value }) is appended to the tool description. Limiting the surface to just two endpoints is done with include: ['createOrder','cancelOrder'] (matched on original operationId) OR by setting every other operation to false / { include: false }. include/exclude still use the original operationId, not the curated name.

task: Two operations end up mapped to the same generated tool name (for example two curated `name` entries collide, or a namePrefix produces a clash). Does createOpenApiTools silently keep one, or fail?
notes: Correct answer: it throws. createOpenApiToolsFromSpec tracks tool names and on a collision throws `Duplicate OpenAPI tool name "<name>" for operations "<opA>" and "<opB>". Use OpenAPI tool curation options to give each generated tool a unique name.` It does NOT silently overwrite or keep the last one.

task: An operation has BOTH a JSON request body AND a real parameter literally named `body`. To avoid the parameter shadowing the body, under what argument key does Smithers place the request body in that case? Answer with just the key.
An operation's requestBody media type is `multipart/form-data` (a file upload). Does the generated Smithers tool actually encode a multipart body, and how does it handle the Content-Type header?
notes: Correct answer (grounded in tool-factory/_helpers.js serializeRequestBody): YES — for a multipart/form-data media type the body is built into a FormData (buildFormData appends each field, and array values are appended item-by-item), and the explicit Content-Type header is DELETED so fetch sets the correct multipart boundary automatically. NOTE: the docs 'Notes/Limitations' incorrectly claim multipart bodies are not encoded; the source does encode them, so an answer saying it is unsupported is wrong. This case surfaces a stale doc.

task: How is an `application/x-www-form-urlencoded` request body serialized by a generated OpenAPI tool, and what Content-Type does it send?
notes: Correct answer (serializeRequestBody/buildUrlEncodedBody): the body object is encoded into a URLSearchParams (each key=value, array values appended per item), and the Content-Type header is set to the urlencoded media type. This is real, working behavior in source even though the docs limitations note understates it.

task: For a request body whose media type is neither JSON nor a form type (e.g. application/octet-stream), how does the generated tool send the body?
notes: Correct answer (serializeRequestBody/buildRawBody): the Content-Type is set to the operation's media type; a Blob, ArrayBuffer, or string body is passed through as-is (raw), and any other value is JSON.stringify'd as a fallback. JSON and +json media types are JSON.stringify'd; form types use FormData / URLSearchParams.

task: An operation's requestBody.content lists both `application/json` and `application/xml`. Which media type does Smithers pick to build the tool's body schema and to serialize the request?
notes: Correct answer: selectRequestBodyContent prefers application/json when it is present in content, regardless of order; only if there is no application/json entry does it fall back to the FIRST content entry. The selected media type drives both the Zod body schema (buildOperationSchema) and the serialization (requestBodyMediaType). So here it picks application/json, not application/xml.

task: My baseUrl is `https://api.example.com/v2` and an operation's path is `/pets`. What full URL does the generated tool call — does the `/v2` base path survive, or does it get discarded?
notes: Correct answer: buildUrl preserves the server base path by joining it with the operation path, so the request goes to https://api.example.com/v2/pets (NOT https://api.example.com/pets). It reads fullUrl.pathname as the base path, strips trailing/leading slashes, and concatenates basePath + '/' + opPath, specifically to avoid new URL discarding the base path when given an absolute operation path.

task: I pass an already-parsed Swagger 2.0 spec object (it has a `swagger: '2.0'` field and no `openapi` field) to createOpenApiTools. What happens, and what should I do instead?
notes: Correct answer: it is rejected. The loaders check a pre-loaded spec OBJECT for an 'openapi' field and, when missing, throw an error explaining only OpenAPI 3.x is supported and that Swagger 2.0 specs (which use a 'swagger' field) must be converted to OpenAPI 3.x first. Smithers does not auto-convert Swagger 2.0; you must supply an OpenAPI 3.x spec with openapi/paths/info.

task: Author a Smithers workflow `incident-triage.tsx` for an on-call scenario. It should: load OpenAPI tools from ./pagerduty.json with a bearer token (include only the read-only incident endpoints), run a triage <Task> agent that summarizes the active incident, then — BEFORE any mutating 'resolve incident' <Task> — require a human <Approval> gate, and put the resolve step behind a retry <Loop> in case the API is flaky. Wrap the whole thing in a top-level <Sequence>.
canonicalAnswer: <Sequence> wrapping a triage <Task> (tools from createOpenApiTools('./pagerduty.json', { auth:{type:'bearer',token}, include:[...] })), then an <Approval> gate, then a retry <Loop> around the resolve-incident <Task>
notes: Graph must render and contain a <Sequence> wrapping the steps, the triage and resolve <Task> nodes, an <Approval> gate before the mutating step, and a <Loop> wrapping the resolve task. Tools are wired via createOpenApiTools with bearer auth + an include allowlist passed into the agent.

task: Author a Smithers workflow `github-digest.tsx` that builds OpenAPI tools from the GitHub spec but excludes all mutating endpoints (use exclude with the write operationIds), then runs two read-only agents in <Parallel> — one <Task> fetches open issues, the other <Task> fetches open PRs — and finally a summarize <Task> folds both results into a digest. Put everything under a top-level <Sequence>.
canonicalAnswer: <Sequence> containing a <Parallel> of two read <Task> nodes (tools from createOpenApiTools with an exclude blocklist of write operationIds) followed by a summarize <Task>
notes: Graph must render and contain a top-level <Sequence>, a <Parallel> block fanning the two read <Task> agents, and a final summarize <Task>. The OpenAPI tools are filtered with the exclude option (matched on original operationId) to keep the surface read-only.

task: Author a production connector workflow `stripe-refunds.tsx`. Context: a finance agent must process a batch of refund requests against a real payments API. Build curated OpenAPI tools from ./stripe.json (bearer auth, renamed + a curated read-only subset for listing charges plus the refund mutation). The workflow should: do all file changes inside a <Worktree>; fan out per-refund investigation across a <Parallel> of investigate <Task> agents; gate each actual refund mutation behind a human <Approval>; and run a reconciliation/review pass over the batch in a <ReviewLoop> before finishing. Compose the phases with a top-level <Sequence>.
canonicalAnswer: <Sequence> containing a <Worktree>, a <Parallel> of investigate <Task> agents (tools from curated createOpenApiTools with bearer auth + operations curation), an <Approval> before each refund mutation, and a <ReviewLoop> reconciliation pass
notes: Genuinely multi-feature/complex: graph must render and contain <Sequence>, <Worktree>, <Parallel>, <Approval>, <Task>, and ideally <ReviewLoop>. OpenAPI tools come from createOpenApiTools with bearer auth and per-operation curation (operations record keyed by original operationId, name/description, include subset). The mutating refund step is human-gated.
